task_completion.description: Load the california_earthquakes.geojson file into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel shows 'california_earthquakes' and point features representing earthquakes are visible on the map canvas.
The 'IDW Interpolation' dialog box is open on the screen (usually accessed via Processing Toolbox).
In the IDW Interpolation dialog, the 'Interpolation attribute' dropdown is set to 'mag' and it has been added to the Input layers list with the type 'Points'.

task_completion.description: Set the pixel size to approximately 0.05 degrees or at least 100x100 pixels
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the IDW Interpolation dialog, 'Pixel size X' and 'Pixel size Y' are set to approximately 0.05, OR 'Rows' and 'Columns' are set to 100 or greater.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting GeoTIFF to the specified exports folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Interpolated' output file path in the dialog is set to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/earthquake_magnitude_surface.tif', or a new raster layer with this name appears in the Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Save the QGIS project to the specified projects folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS window title bar shows 'earthquake_interpolation' or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/earthquake_interpolation.qgz'.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS graphical user interface to perform the interpolation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QGIS application window and its dialogs being interacted with, rather than terminal commands like gdal_grid.

integrity.description: The output raster is a genuine interpolation result
integrity.visual_evidence: The map canvas displays a continuous raster surface (usually a grayscale or colored gradient) representing the interpolated magnitudes, bounded by the point data extent.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or terminal to create dummy files
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim) or terminal 'touch' commands are visible creating the .tif or .qgz files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: /home/ga/GIS_Data/california_earthquakes.geojson. Expected output file: /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/earthquake_magnitude_surface.tif. Expected project file: /home/ga/GIS_Data/projects/earthquake_interpolation.qgz. The interpolation must use the 'mag' attribute. The resulting raster should represent values between approximately 2.5 and 5.1 (the min/max magnitudes of the input data).
